paragraphs.body: After I stepped onto the land of America and started my college life in Pittsburgh, I realized a lot of differences between the US and my Homeland of China, as well as between my expectations of Pittsburgh and the reality of the city. These differences evoked me all kinds of feelings. Some of them made me feel embarrassed, such as the time when I took the bus for the first time and didn't realize that I needed to pull the string to request a stop. As a result, I missed my stop and ended up walking for a long time to reach my destination. But the difference I am going to discuss, which is a comparison between the Kennywood in Pittsburgh and the Happy Valley in Qingdao, China, brings me more surprise.

First of all, Kennywood gave me a lot of new experiences that I never had in any amusement park in China. Last year when I was invited by my friends to go to Kennywood, it was imagined as a small amusement park for little kids, with warming and lovely decorations for children(In the left picture below). I thought the amusement accommodations were all unintimidating and might be boring for me, who is a big fan of roller coasters and other exciting programs. But its true self gave me a big surprise. The first characteristic I noticed which was so different from my expectations was the decoration in the park and the clothing of the staff, which together created a spooky Halloween atmosphere(in the right picture below). Also, all of the entertainment programs, such as the high-speed roller coasters, were very thrilling and exciting, which gave me great experiences.

There are also so many distinguishments between Happy Valley in my hometown, Qingdao, China, and Kennywood in Pittsburgh. The Happy Valley is always very crowded because Qingdao is a very popular city for traveling because of its beautiful beaches and mountains. For popular programs such as roller coaster or pirate ship(海盗船), the wait time always exceeds two hours. But Kennywood was not so crowded even during the Halloween period and during the weekends. The lines of most programs are not so long so that I can try all of the programs in half of the day. There are also many common advantages of Happy Valley and Kennywood. For instance, both amusement parks are very large with many different programs, satisfying all kinds of interests and preferences.
The AI-generated picture of Happy Valley
